title: uprmputh
html:
uprmputh

Attach a parameter to an operation.

long uprmputh (UHACT hOpr, int seqNr, UHACT pParam);

Parameters

hOpr
The handle to the operation
seqNr
The sequence number of the parameter (the sequence starts with 1, not zero)
pParam
The handle of the (basic or entity) parameter that must be attached to the operation

Return Values

For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic.

Use

Library: ucall

Description

Use uprmputh to pass parameter data from one operation to another. Suppose operation A has an entity output parameter, and operation B has the same entity as an input parameter. After calling A, you can then detach the parameter from A (by calling uprmgeth with the Detach parameter set to TRUE), and simply attach it to B (by calling uprmputh), instead of having to loop through all fields in all occurrences.

Although this method is useful mostly for entity parameters, it can also be used for basic parameters, if your 3GL code is not interested in the actual value. For a string parameter, this would save you from having to free the memory occupied by the string, because there is no need to call uuf2str.

Example: uprmgeth and uprmputh

This example shows how to pass an entity parameter from one service to another without the 3GL code actually seeing the data.

Note: For an example of how to use this function with an operation parameter, see the example given with uoprprms.

In this example, there are two operations, one called GETENT and one called SETENT. They are in the same component ENTSRV, but they could be in different components, as well. The operation GETENT returns entity data for the entity ENT by means of an output entity parameter. Similarly, operation SETENT accepts this same data by means of an input entity parameter. The entity ENT is defined as:

PK char(3)
REC char(20)

The two operations are defined as follows:

operation GETENT ; Return 3 occurrences of ENT
params
entity ent : out;
endparams
creocc "ent", -1 ; Create them one by one
pk = "1"
rec = "Rec1 %%$clock"
creocc "ent", -1
pk = "2"
rec = "Rec2 %%$clock"
creocc "ent", -1
pk = "3"
rec = "Rec3 %%$clock"
end

operation SETENT ; Accept 3 occurrences of ENT
params
entity ent : in;
endparams
putmess "PK REC "; And print them to the messageframe
putmess "=== ====================";
setocc "ent", 1
putmess "%%pk %%rec"
setocc "ent", 2
putmess "%%pk %%rec"
setocc "ent", 3
putmess "%%pk %%rec"
end

The following 3GL code activates operation GETENT to retrieve the entity data, then passes the same data on to operation SETENT, just by using the entity handle. Note that we call uprmgeth to detach the handle from GETENT, then we call uprmputh to attach the handle to operation SETENT.

#include <h3gl.h>
void get_ent ()
{
UHACT hEnv; /* Environment handle */
UHACT hIn; /* Instance ENTSRV */
UHACT hOp1, hOp2; /* Operations GETENT and SETENT */
UHACT hEnt; /* Entity handle */
long actStatus; /* Activation status */
long procErr; /* Activation procerr */

ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */
uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"ENTSRV", 0, 0,
UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr);/* Instantiate ENTSRV service*/

uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETENT", 0, &hOp1);
uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"SETENT", 0, &hOp2);

uopract (hOp1, &actStatus, &procErr);/* Activate GETENT (fills ent data*/

uprmgeth (hOp1, 1, 1, &hEnt); /* Detach entity parameter from GETENT*/
uprmputh (hOp2, 1, hEnt); /* Attach entity parameter to SETENT*/

uopract (hOp2, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Pass entity data on to SETENT */

uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */
ufreeh (&hOp1); /* Free GETENT operation handle */
ufreeh (&hOp2); /* Free SETENT operation handle */
ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */
ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */
}

Note the following points when detaching parameters:

  • Fields can not be detached or attached.
  • The name of the entity parameter does not have to be the same in both operations. What does need to be the same, because uprmputh will check it, is the number and data type of the fields.
  • You can only detach a parameter when there are no handles that refer to it. In the above code, we did not have a handle to the entity. But we would have had, if the 3GL code also wanted to display the data. In that case, we would have needed to call ufree(&hEnt;) before detaching the parameter with uprmgeth.
  • Uniface keeps a reference count with each handle. Data belonging to the handle is kept until the last reference is freed. This means, for example, that you can free an operation handle and still refer to the data in one of the parameters of that operation. That data is released only when you free the parameter handle.
Related Links
  • Call-In API Return Codes

    Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise.

  • uprmgeth

    Get the handle of the specified operation parameter or entity field. Or detach a parameter from an operation.
